exercises.title: Consonant to Vowel
exercises.instruction: Connect words smoothly. When a word ends with a consonant and the next starts with a vowel, link them: pick‿it‿up.
tips: Say it as one continuous word: pick-it-up

exercises.title: Same Consonant Links
exercises.instruction: When words have the same consonant sound at the boundary, say the sound only once: goo(d) deal.
tips: Don't say 'good-duh' - just hold the d sound longer
The two 'd' sounds merge into one longer 'd'
Keep the rhythm smooth and connected

exercises.title: Linked Thought Groups
exercises.instruction: Break this into thought groups and link words within each group.
exercises.targetText: I'll send it over, and then you can check it out.
chunks: and then you can check it out.
tips: Link within each chunk, not between chunks

exercises.title: Stress with Linking
exercises.instruction: Keep the stress pattern clear while linking words smoothly.
tips: Emphasize 'turn' and 'on' - make them louder and longer
